DAMAGE TO HANGER AT WILL ROGERS AIRPORT.

DELAYED REPORT. THE MOST CONCENTRATED AREA OF DAMAGE EXTENDS FROM THE WEST SIDE OF WILL ROGERS WORLD AIRPORT TO THE 2100-2800 BLOCK OF S. WESTERN AVE. THIS INCLUDES POWER POLES, POWER LINES, TREES, AND SIGNS BLOWN DOWN. SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WITH WINDOWS BLOWN OUT IN HOMES AND BUSINESSES. DAMAGE ALONG WESTERN AVE EXTENDS NORTH TO 21ST AVE. DAMAGE ALSO REPORTED ON AGNEW, SW 55TH, AND SOUTH BARNES. ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS AT LEAST 70-75 MPH. ESTIMATED TIME FROM ABOUT 12:05 TO ABOUT 12:25 AM CDT. PRELIMINARY ASSESSMENT OF RADAR AND ENVIRONMENTAL DATA SUGGESTS DAMAGING RFD WINDS, BUT THE INVESTIGATION CONTINUES.

THE TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTHEAST NORMAN TO THE SOUTHWEST OF 24TH AVENUE NORTHEAST AND TECUMSEH ROAD. IT MOVED EAST AND NORTHEAST TO SOUTH OF SOUTHEAST 149TH STREET AND ANDERSON ROAD IN SOUTHEAST OKLAHOMA CITY. THERE WERE NUMEROUS TREES AND FENCES DOWNED ALONG THE PATH. AT LEAST TWO BARNS HAD SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE, AND AT LEAST A COUPLE OF HOMES HAD DAMAGE TO A CARPORT OR AWNING.
